volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Little Carthaginian (Section 5.5.1320 - Section 5.7.1385)

sections.label: Section 5.5.1320
sections.translation: bring out some cudgels !

sections.label: Section 5.5.1320b
sections.original: Heus tu , si quid per iocum
sections.translation: Hark you , if I have said anything in a joke , don ’ t you be for taking it seriously .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1322
sections.original: Qui tibi lubido est , opsecro , Antamonides ,
sections.translation: Prithee , what pleasure have you , Anthemonides , in speaking rudely to our kinsman and father ? For this is our father ; he has just now recognized us ,

sections.label: Section 5.5.1325
sections.translation: and him as the son of his cousin .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1325b
sections.translation: So may Jupiter kindly bless me , I heartily rejoice that it is so , and I am delighted , if , in fact , any great misfortune befalls this Procurer , and since a fortune awaits you equal to your merits .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1329
sections.original: Credibile ecastor dicit . crede huic , mi pater .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , he says what ’ s worthy of belief ; do believe him , my father .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1330_1331
sections.translation: I do believe him .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1330_1331b
sections.original: Et ego credo . sed eccum lenonem Lycum .
sections.translation: And I believe him . But look ( pointing .) I espy the Procurer Lycus ,

sections.label: Section 5.5.1332
sections.original: Bonum virum eccum video , se recipit domum ;
sections.translation: the worthy fellow ; look , there he is — he ’ s betaking himself homeward .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1333
sections.translation: Who is this ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.1333b
sections.original: Vtrumvis est , vel leno vel Lycus .
sections.translation: He ’ s which you please , both the Procurer and Lycus . He has been keeping your daughters in servitude ,

sections.label: Section 5.5.1335
sections.translation: and from myself he has stolen some gold .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1335b
sections.translation: A pretty fellow for you to be acquainted with !

sections.label: Section 5.5.1336
sections.translation: Let ’ s bring him to justice .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1336b
sections.translation: By no means .

sections.label: Section 5.5.1336c
sections.translation: For what reason ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.1336d
sections.translation: Because ’ twere better for an action of damages to be brought against him [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1338
sections.translation: No one , in my opinion at least , is deceived , who rightly states his case to his friends .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1340
sections.original: nam omnibus amicis meis idem unum convenit ,
sections.translation: But by all my friends the one same thing is agreed upon , that I ought to hang myself , so as not to be adjudged to Agorastocles .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1342
sections.translation: Procurer , let ’ s away to the court of justice .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1342b
sections.translation: I do entreat you , Agorastocles , that I may be at liberty to hang myself .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1343b
sections.translation: I summon you to justice .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1344
sections.translation: But what have you to do with me ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.1344b
sections.translation: Because I affirm that both of these are my daughters , free women , and free by birth ,

sections.label: Section 5.6.1346
sections.original: eae súnt surruptae cum nutrice parvolae .
sections.translation: who , when little , were kidnapped together with their nurse .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1347
sections.original: Iam pridem equidem istuc scivi , et miratus fui ,
sections.translation: Indeed , I knew that already , and I wondered that no one came to assert their freedom ; they really are none of mine , indeed .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1349b
sections.translation: Procurer , you must come to justice .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1350
sections.original: De prandio tu dicis . debetur , dabo .
sections.translation: You are talking about the breakfast ; it is owing to you ; I ’ ll give it .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1351
sections.original: Duplum pro furto mi opus est .
sections.translation: Twofold compensation I must have for the theft .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1351b
sections.translation: Take it out of this , then .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1352
sections.translation: And I require a full satisfaction .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1352b
sections.translation: Take out of this whatever you please .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1353
sections.translation: And I , indeed , a mina of silver .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1353b
sections.translation: Take out of this whatever you please . I ’ ll at once settle the matter for all with my neck , just like a porter .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1355
sections.translation: Do you refuse me in any way ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.1355b
sections.translation: Not a word , in fact .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1356
sections.original: Ite igitur intro , mulieres . sed patrue mi ,
sections.translation: Go in-doors , then , damsels . But ( to HANNO .) my kinsman , betroth me your daughter , as you promised .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1358
sections.translation: I should not venture to do otherwise .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1358b
sections.translation: Kindly farewell !

sections.label: Section 5.6.1358c
sections.translation: And kindly farewell to you !

sections.label: Section 5.6.1359
sections.original: Leno , arrabonem hoc pro mina mecum fero .—
sections.translation: Procurer , I take this as a pledge with me for my mina .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1360
sections.translation: By heavens , I am ruined !

sections.label: Section 5.6.1360b
sections.original: Immo haud multo post , si in ius veneris .
sections.translation: Why yes , before very long , when you ’ ve come to justice .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1361
sections.original: Quin egomet tibi me addico . quid praetore opust ?
sections.translation: Nay but , I own myself your slave . What need of the Praetor have we ? But I beseech you that I may be allowed to pay the simple sum [*] , three hundred Philippeans . I think it can be scraped together ; to-morrow I ’ ll have an auction .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1364b
sections.translation: On condition , then ,

sections.label: Section 5.6.1365
sections.original: ut sis apud me lignea in custodia .
sections.translation: that you shall be in wooden custody at my house .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1366
sections.translation: So be it .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1366b
sections.original: Sequere intro , patrue mi , út hunc festum diem
sections.translation: Follow me in-doors , my kinsman , that we may keep this festive day in joyousness , upon his misfortune and our good fortune . ( To the AUDIENCE .) Heartily fare you well . To great length have we gone ; at last all these misfortunes fall upon the Procurer .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1370
sections.original: nunc , quod postremum est condimentum fabulae ,
sections.translation: Now — that which is the last seasoning for our Play — if it has pleased you , our Comedy asks applause .

sections.label: Section 5.7.1372
sections.original: Quam rém agis , miles ? qui lubet patruo meo
sections.translation: What is it you are about , Captain ? Why does it please you to speak rudely to my relative ? Don ’ t be surprised that the damsels do follow after him ; he has just now discovered that both of them are his own daughters .

sections.label: Section 5.7.1375b
sections.translation: Hah ! what speech was it that reached my ears ? Now I am undone !

sections.label: Section 5.7.1376b
sections.translation: ( To AGORASTOCLES .) From what house were these females lost ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.1377
sections.translation: They are Carthaginians .

sections.label: Section 5.7.1377b
sections.translation: Then I am ruined . I was always in dread of that , lest some one should recognize them , a thing which has now come to pass . Woe unto wretched me !

sections.label: Section 5.7.1380
sections.translation: My eighteen minae are lost , I guess , which I paid for them .

sections.label: Section 5.7.1381b
sections.translation: And you yourself are lost , Lycus .

sections.label: Section 5.7.1382
sections.translation: Who is this ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.1382b
sections.original: Vtrumvis est , vel leno vel Lycus .
sections.translation: Which you please , he ’ s either the Procurer or Lycus . He has been keeping your daughters in servitude , and from myself he has stolen some gold .

sections.label: Section 5.7.1384b
sections.translation: A pretty fellow for you to be acquainted with !

sections.label: Section 5.7.1385
sections.original: Leno , rapacem te esse semper credidi ,
sections.translation: Procurer , I always deemed you to be avaricious , but they know you to be a thief as well , who know more of you .
